summary: The case reveals a cognitive-behavioral pattern of repetitive message-checking driven by anticipatory fear of negative social evaluation, leading to delayed replies and potential misinterpretation by others. Six modifiable levers across cognitive-behavioral strategies, social communication skills, and bio-physiological factors target the core mechanisms (C01–C06): reducing excessive proofreading and indecision (C01, C05), easing fear of judgment (C02), shortening reply latency (C03), correcting perceived social negative feedback (C04), and improving time-related functioning (C06). Each lever has a concrete predictor, measurable outcomes, and a practical time horizon, with safety notes where relevant.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured send-Ready checklist + time-bound sending rule
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a brief 'send-ready' criterion and a 2-minute drafting window: after drafting, use a simple checklist (tone, clarity, empathy). If criteria are met, send; if not, revise using approved templates before sending.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalizes the criteria for adequacy, reducing over-checking and uncertainty about message suitability, while providing a fast-path to sending and reducing delay.
solutions.measurement: Avg. edits per outgoing message; average time from drafting to sending; frequency of checklist use; self-reported send-readiness.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S01 to C01 (reduce proofreading), C02 (address fear via structure), C05 (clarify/standardize 'good enough'), and C03 (reduce delay) through externalized criteria.

solutions.predictor_variable: Graduated exposure to social evaluation in messaging
solutions.solution_candidate: A stepped exposure plan starting with low-stakes messages that require minimal editing, gradually increasing complexity. Include reflective brief after each step to normalize responses and reduce avoidance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Systematic exposure reduces anticipatory anxiety, decreases avoidance, and recalibrates perceived social cost of delays, which can lower proofreading and delay.
solutions.measurement: Self-reported anxiety before/after tasks (0-10 scale); number of messages sent with minimal checking; perceived social feedback after messages.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S02 to C02 (anxiety about judgment), C04 (perceived social cost), C03 (delay), and C01 (checking)

solutions.predictor_variable: Templates + AI-assisted prompts for message drafting
solutions.solution_candidate: Create a library of concise, safe templates and prompts to help draft messages quickly; use prompts to check tone and clarity before sending.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: External templates reduce cognitive load and uncertainty about adequacy, decreasing proofreading and improving confidence in send-worthiness.
solutions.measurement: Template usage frequency; average edits per message; time to draft; self-rated readiness to send.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S03 to C01 (reduce checking), C05 (internal standard for 'good enough'), C02 (fear of sounding stupid) via templates.

solutions.predictor_variable: Mindfulness-based affect regulation for messaging-related anxiety
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily brief mindfulness or acceptance-based practices to reduce anticipatory anxiety and rumination about messages, improving tolerance for imperfect communication.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced affect regulation lowers reactivity to perceived social evaluation, reduces pre-message arousal, and supports calmer decision-making about when to send.
solutions.measurement: Daily mindfulness practice adherence; pre/post-message anxiety scores; latency to reply; perceived interpretation by others.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S04 to C02 (fear/anxiety), C04 (perceived social feedback), C06 (time/attention regulation)

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured coaching with role-plays to enhance clarity, warmth, and appropriate reply pacing to reduce fear of judgment and improve timely responses.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved communication skills reduce perceived social threat and misinterpretation, enabling faster and more confident replies.
solutions.measurement: Self-rated confidence; observed changes in message tone/clarity; response latency; satisfaction with social interactions.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S05 to C02 (fear of judgment), C04 (perceived feedback), C03 (delays)

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep hygiene and circadian stability to support cognitive control
solutions.solution_candidate: Establish regular sleep schedules, consistent wake times, and winding-down routines to improve executive function and emotional regulation related to messaging.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep enhances cognitive control and emotional regulation, reducing excessive checking and improving timing of replies.
solutions.measurement: Bedtime/wake time consistency (hours), sleep quality rating, proofreading frequency per message, average response time.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S06 to C02 (anxiety), C01 (checking), C06 (time efficiency)
